canonical_question: How are stock options taxed — ISO vs. NSO, AMT, 83(b) election, cross-border?
aliases: stock options AMT alternative minimum tax
constraints: US federal tax rules only — 7 states impose state AMT (CA, CO, CT, IA, MN, NY, WI)
OBBBA (July 2025) permanently extended higher AMT exemptions but reverted phaseout thresholds
ISOs W-2 employees of corporations only; contractors/advisors get NSOs
83(b) election irrevocable with strict 30-day deadline; does not apply to RSUs
Cross-border taxation requires bilateral treaty analysis per jurisdiction pair

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about equity compensation accounting (ASC 718 / IFRS 2)
User is asking about 409A valuation methodology for setting strike price
User is asking about ESPP (Employee Stock Purchase Plan) taxation

inputs_needed.question: What type of equity compensation does the user hold?
In which country is the user a tax resident?
What stage is the user at?
options: At sale (selling shares, need to know tax treatment)

applies_to.user_segment: US taxpayers receiving employee stock options, startup founders, equity compensation recipients, cross-border employees
applies_to.context: Federal income tax treatment of incentive stock options (ISOs) and non-qualified stock options (NSOs) under IRC Sections 421-424 and 83
